label: Spotify Search API
summary: Spotify search is a legitimate SongMetadata fallback because the official endpoint supports structured filters including artist, track, and isrc.
It is still clearly weaker than direct Spotify track-id lookup because search ambiguity now becomes part of the adapter's correctness burden.
So this row should remain visible as a real candidate, but it should sit below direct-id enrichment in both product trust and implementation order.

sources.label: GitHub: Spotify Web API TS SDK

signals: Spotify search supports field filters including artist, track, and isrc.
Healthy JS SDK families exist, so implementation mechanics are not the blocker.
The real product risk is search ambiguity rather than raw API capability.

notes: The adapter depends on Spotify's hosted Web API and platform rules.

entries.subject: Spotify Search API
notes: The adapter depends on Spotify Web API access and Spotify platform policy.

entries.subject: spotify/spotify-web-api-ts-sdk

evidence.label: GitHub: Spotify Web API TS SDK

notes: Official SDK evidence exists for a typed implementation path.
The API and JS ecosystem are strong.
Search-policy correctness and ambiguity handling are the real maintenance cost.

methods.notes: Feasible, but auth plus search-result ambiguity keep this from being a clean browser-first recommendation.
There is no engine to port.
A native bridge is unnecessary for a search-only metadata API unless it already owns credentials.
Possible, but not a natural fit for a search-auth problem.
Too brittle for an authenticated provider search lane.
A companion can own credentials, but a shared host is still cleaner.
Technically fine, but Node/TypeScript still has the cleaner evidence here.
Best immediate fit: structured query construction, result filtering, and auth ownership all sit comfortably here.
Adds indirection to an already ambiguous search path.
A shared broker is a good place to centralize search-policy heuristics and caching.
Viable, but heavier than the current product need.
This is a web API search path, not a platform SDK story.

notes: This is a legitimate fallback only after direct Spotify id lookup is unavailable.
Keep it bounded by provider affinity and strict winner selection.

openQuestions: How much search ambiguity is acceptable before SongMetadata should refuse to enrich instead of guessing?
Should Spotify search be allowed to use ISRC filters first when that field is present, before trying artist/title text?
